en: A wise son hears his father's instruction, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
zh: 智慧的兒子聽從父親的教訓， 好譏笑人的不聽責備。

en: From the fruit of his mouth a man eats what is good, but the desire of the treacherous is for violence.
zh: 人因口所結的果子，必得嘗美物， 奸詐人的慾望卻是強暴。

en: Whoever guards his mouth preserves his life; he who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.
zh: 謹慎口舌的，可保性命； 口沒遮攔的，自取滅亡。

en: The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied.
zh: 懶惰人渴求，卻一無所得； 殷勤人必得豐裕。

en: The righteous hates falsehood, but the wicked brings shame and disgrace.
zh: 義人恨惡虛假， 惡人行事，遺臭貽羞。

en: Righteousness guards him whose way is blameless, but sin overthrows the wicked.
zh: 正義護衛行為完全的人； 邪惡卻使犯罪的人傾覆。

en: One pretends to be rich, yet has nothing; another pretends to be poor, yet has great wealth.
zh: 有人自充富足，卻一無所有； 有人假裝貧窮，財物卻極多。

en: The ransom of a man's life is his wealth, but a poor man hears no threat.
zh: 人的財富是他性命的贖價， 窮人卻聽不見威嚇的話。

en: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
zh: 義人的光必明亮， 惡人的燈必熄滅。

en: By insolence comes nothing but strife, but with those who take advice is wisdom.
zh: 傲慢只能引起爭端； 接受勸告的卻有智慧。

en: Wealth gained hastily will dwindle, but whoever gathers little by little will increase it.
zh: 不勞而獲的財物，必快減少； 慢慢積蓄的，必然增多。

en: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life.
zh: 所盼望的遲延未得，令人心憂； 所願的來到，好比生命樹。

en: Whoever despises the word brings destruction on himself, but he who reveres the commandment will be rewarded.
zh: 藐視訓言的，必自取滅亡； 敬畏誡命的，必得善報。

en: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, that one may turn away from the snares of death.
zh: 智慧人的訓誨是生命的泉源， 能使人避開死亡的網羅。

en: Good sense wins favor, but the way of the treacherous is their ruin.
zh: 聰穎的明慧使人蒙恩， 奸詐人的道路崎嶇難行。

en: In everything the prudent acts with knowledge, but a fool flaunts his folly.
zh: 精明的人都按知識行事， 愚昧人卻顯露自己的愚妄。

en: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
zh: 奸惡的使者，必陷在禍患裡 ； 忠心的使臣，使人復原。

en: Poverty and disgrace come to him who ignores instruction, but whoever heeds reproof is honored.
zh: 輕忽管教的，必致窮乏受辱； 看重責備的，必得尊榮。

en: A desire fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but to turn away from evil is an abomination to fools.
zh: 所願的成功，使人心裡甘甜； 遠離惡事，卻為愚昧人憎惡。

en: Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm.
zh: 與智慧人同行的，必得智慧； 與愚昧人為友的，必受虧損。

en: Disaster pursues sinners, but the righteous are rewarded with good.
zh: 禍患追趕罪人， 義人必得善報。

en: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, but the sinner's wealth is laid up for the righteous.
zh: 善人給子孫留下產業， 罪人的財富，卻是為義人積藏。

en: The fallow ground of the poor would yield much food, but it is swept away through injustice.
zh: 窮人的耕地可產大量糧食， 卻因不義都被摧毀了。

en: Whoever spares the rod hates his son, but he who loves him is diligent to discipline him.
zh: 不用杖責打兒子的，是恨惡他； 愛兒子的，必對他勤加管教。

en: The righteous has enough to satisfy his appetite, but the belly of the wicked suffers want.
zh: 義人必吃得飽足， 惡人卻空著肚子。
